name: Zep AI
pricing_notes: Community (self-hosted, free); Cloud free tier (2K users); Pro $49/mo; Enterprise custom

auth.notes: Project-level API key from Zep Cloud dashboard; self-hosted uses configured secret

performance.notes: Memory retrieval ~80ms; fact extraction runs async after conversation turn; search <200ms

agent_readiness.notes: Long-term memory for LLM applications. Extracts facts, preferences, and summaries from conversations. Hybrid memory: episodic (raw messages), semantic (facts), and temporal (time-aware). LangChain, LlamaIndex, and direct SDK integration. User and session management. Knowledge graph extraction. Better than naive vector store memory for conversational agents.

gotchas: Memory extraction is async — facts may not be immediately available after message ingestion
Fact extraction quality depends on underlying LLM model configured
Not designed for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) — use dedicated vector DBs for that
best_when: Conversational agents needing persistent user memory across sessions
Customer service bots that should remember user preferences and history
Personal assistant agents accumulating knowledge about individual users
Multi-turn agents where session summaries help manage context length
avoid_when: Document/knowledge-base RAG (use Chroma, Pinecone, Weaviate instead)
Stateless single-turn API calls with no user sessions
Teams unable to manage async fact extraction timing
summary: Zep AI provides persistent long-term memory for LLM applications through hybrid memory: episodic (raw messages), semantic (extracted facts), and temporal (time-aware) storage. Automatically extracts facts and preferences from conversations. Python/TypeScript SDKs with LangChain integration. Best for conversational agents that need to remember users across sessions. Distinct from RAG — optimized for user memory, not knowledge retrieval.
